summary:
I have found and fixed the bug. The issue was an off-by-one error in `processor.py` when determining the slice indices for processing batches of `records`.

In Python, slicing `records[start:end]` is right-exclusive, meaning the element at index `end` is **not included** in the result. By calculating `end = start + batch_size - 1`, the original code was inadvertently dropping the last record of every batch (i.e. returning a batch of `batch_size - 1` items).

I changed the indices to `end = start + batch_size`. Python natively handles cases where `end` exceeds the length of the list by capping it at the list's boundary, so this fix correctly processes all the records across batches. 

The `/app/test.sh` verification suite now passes successfully.
